title: allium curtum subsp. curtum
intro: Allium curtum subsp. curtum, also known as the wild garlic, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its strong onion-like smell and edible bulbs. The medicinal properties of this plant include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with consuming wild garlic include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of wild garlic include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
